title: Tribal Affairs
summary: This Treasury.gov page describes the Office of Tribal and Native Affairs, which was established in September 2022 to advise on Tribal policy, coordinate Tribal consultations, and manage the Treasury Tribal Advisory Committee (TTAC). The content is administrative in nature and focuses on Tribal government relations, economic programs, and community support—not on financial market structure or macro economic catalysts.
key_points: Office of Tribal and Native Affairs established September 2022 and is overseen by the U.S. Treasurer
Office advises on Tribal policy, coordinates Tribal consultations, and manages the Treasury Tribal Advisory Committee (TTAC)
TTAC is a seven-member advisory committee that advises the Secretary on Tribal tax matters pursuant to the Tribal General Welfare Exclusion Act of 2014
The Office works across Treasury and IRS offices on economic programs, tax policies, and matters impacting Tribal and Native communities
Content is administrative and focuses on government-Tribal relations, not financial markets or macro catalysts
